event: found lying at home with her family
transported to our hospital by ambulance
hemorrhage in bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
hemorrhage was confined within the ventricles
spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
blood pressure control was initiated in the intensive care unit
cerebral angiograph revealed an aneurysm at the distal site of the mLSA
arising from the proximal A1 segment of the left anterior cerebral artery
no signs of hemorrhagic complications on the postoperative head CT
no signs of cerebral infarction on the postoperative head CT
neither impaired consciousness nor paralysis developed
lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricle wall
decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
